name: Multiwoven Open-Source Reverse ETL API
what_it_does: Open-source reverse ETL platform with REST API — enabling data teams to sync data from data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) to business tools (CRM, marketing, support) with model-based SQL queries, connector mappings, and sync scheduling, as an open-source alternative to Hightouch and Census.
use_cases: Syncing warehouse data via Multiwoven API for reverse ETL to Salesforce, HubSpot, and marketing tools
Managing data models via Multiwoven API for SQL query-based data activation
Configuring sync destinations via Multiwoven API for infrastructure-as-code connector management
Monitoring sync jobs via Multiwoven API for pipeline health and error tracking
Scheduling data activations via Multiwoven API for time-based warehouse-to-destination syncs
not_for: Source-to-warehouse ETL (use Airbyte or Fivetran for that direction)
Real-time CDC streaming (Multiwoven is scheduled batch sync)
Teams without a data warehouse (Multiwoven requires a warehouse as the source)
best_when: A data team wants an open-source, self-hosted reverse ETL platform to sync warehouse data to business tools — avoiding Hightouch/Census pricing while maintaining control over the infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need managed SaaS, real-time streaming, or a platform with 200+ pre-built connectors.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Multiwoven account settings. REST API at {your-instance}/api/v1/ for self-hosted or api.multiwoven.com for cloud. Workspace ID required in API paths. Ruby on Rails backend. Self-hosted via Docker Compose or Kubernetes. Multiwoven Cloud: managed SaaS option.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source: free self-hosted with community connectors
pricing.paid_tiers: Multiwoven Cloud: custom pricing; Enterprise support: custom
pricing.notes: US company. Founded 2023. Apache 2.0 license. Open-source alternative to Hightouch and Census. Built with Ruby on Rails backend, React frontend. Connectors: growing library of source connectors (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks) and destination connectors (Salesforce, HubSpot, Stripe, Google Ads, etc.). Models: SQL query templates that define the dataset to sync. Syncs: orchestration layer connecting models to destinations. Custom connectors: extensible connector framework. Docker Compose: quick local deployment. Kubernetes: production deployment. Community: Discord, GitHub Issues. Early-stage: fewer connectors than established players.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Workspace ID in paths. Self-hosted or Multiwoven Cloud. Early-stage (founded 2023). Apache 2.0. Ruby on Rails. Docker Compose/K8s deployment. Growing connector library.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sync reads idempotent by sync ID. Model creation: check if model exists before creating. Sync triggers: idempotent if sync is not already running.
known_agent_gotchas: Early-stage platform (2023): API surface and connectors actively evolving — expect breaking changes
Workspace ID required in all API paths — obtain from account settings
Self-hosted deployment: Docker Compose for dev, Kubernetes for production — separate setup required
Connector library: smaller than Hightouch/Census — verify your destination is supported before adopting
Model-based sync: SQL query defines the dataset — models are the core unit of data activation
Multiwoven Cloud: managed option available but self-hosted is the primary open-source value proposition

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS via your infrastructure (self-hosted — operator responsibility). Bearer token. Apache 2.0: auditable codebase. GDPR-friendly: self-hosted keeps data in your environment. Destination credentials stored in Multiwoven — warehouse access + destination API keys. Early-stage: fewer security audits. Self-hosted: operator controls all access. Ruby on Rails: monitor for Rails CVEs. No built-in secrets management — store connector credentials securely.
